- High cohesion (0.87) — files are tightly interconnected

- Large zone size (137 files) reflects comprehensive platform functionality including CLI, MCP servers, and dashboard components
- Schema files and public API exports indicate well-structured interface boundaries for external consumers
- Comprehensive platform implementation with good internal organization despite managing substantial scope
- Implements inverse dependency pattern by importing FROM specialized zones rather than being imported BY them
- Inverse dependency architecture where platform layer composes from specialized zones creates plugin-like extensibility
- Heavy incoming dependencies from web-16 (13 imports) suggest potential violation of platform abstraction boundaries
- 137-file zone size violates single responsibility principle and indicates need for architectural decomposition
- Massive zone size (137 files) creates maintainability burden and violates focused responsibility principle - should be decomposed into 4-6 cohesive sub-zones
- Gateway pattern implementation (domain-gateway.ts, rex-gateway.ts) represents architectural abstraction not adopted by other zones with external dependencies
- Gateway pattern success in web-platform should be considered for agent-core's external dependencies to improve architectural consistency
- Web package exhibits god-zone anti-pattern where primary web zone (137 files) acts as catch-all while specialized concerns fragment into 28 micro-zones, inverting expected architectural hierarchy
- Web package exhibits systematic architectural debt requiring comprehensive refactoring strategy rather than incremental fixes
- Implement three-phase web package consolidation: Phase 1 - merge zones web-2,web-10,web-11,web-13 (shared coupling patterns), Phase 2 - consolidate visualization zones web-14,web-16,web-17,web-24, Phase 3 - extract shared UI foundation from primary web zone
- Primary web zone acts as architectural sink, accumulating 137 files while proper domain separation fragments into 28+ micro-zones, indicating inverted architectural hierarchy
